name: United States v. Diaco
summary: A pre-Sentencing Reform Act case where a district court granted early release after the BOP filed a motion partly based on sentencing disparities caused by another court's ultra vires actions affecting co-defendants.
significance: Cited as one of only two published opinions under the predecessor compassionate release statute (Section 4205(g)), where the BOP moved for release based in part on sentencing disparities rather than solely personal circumstances.
